reforms.description: In 2008, Korea introduced an earned income tax credit (EITC) aimed at low-income workers, particularly those with children, to promote employment and reduce poverty.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The EITC is expected to increase labor supply and reduce poverty, thereby enhancing overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The EITC was introduced and is expected to benefit a significant number of households.
reforms.importance_rationale: The EITC represents a significant policy shift towards supporting low-income workers, with potential positive effects on employment and income distribution.
reforms.source_quote: In 2008, Korea introduced an earned income tax credit (EITC), an in-work tax credit used in a number of OECD countries.

reforms.description: The government introduced a 'Basic Plan to Promote Employment of the Aged' aimed at increasing the employment rate of older workers, which includes encouraging firms to raise their mandatory retirement age and providing wage subsidies for retaining older employees.
reforms.package_name: Basic Plan to Promote Employment of the Aged
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the employment of older workers, the reform aims to enhance the labor force participation rate and leverage the experience of older employees, contributing to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan is set to continue through 2021 and has already been initiated.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in labor market policy aimed at addressing demographic challenges and improving employment rates among older workers, which is crucial for sustaining economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s 'Basic Plan to Promote Employment of the Aged' aims at increasing the employment rate of the 55-to-64 age group from 59% in 2005 to at least 63%.

reforms.description: The government announced 'Service PROGRESS-I' in April 2008 to improve Korea's service account balance, focusing on areas such as tourism, medical care, and knowledge-based services, with plans for increased financial support and regulatory easing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing support and reducing regulatory barriers, the initiative aims to stimulate growth in the service sector, thereby boosting overall productivity and economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced a roadmap for the service sector in April 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change aimed at revitalizing the service sector, which is crucial for economic competitiveness and productivity growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced 'Service PROGRESS-I' in April 2008 to improve Korea's service account balance, focusing on deficit areas such as tourism, medical care, overseas language training and knowledge-based services.

reforms.description: The government plans to double the share of government R&D in industrial technology that goes to the service sector from 3.1% in 2008 to 6.2% in 2012, aiming to enhance innovation in this area.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding for the service sector is expected to foster innovation and improve productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The share of government R&D in industrial technology that goes to the service sector is to be doubled.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it reallocates resources to a sector that has been historically underfunded, thereby enhancing its potential for innovation and growth.
reforms.source_quote: The share of government R&D in industrial technology that goes to the service sector is to be doubled from 3.1% in 2008 to 6.2% in 2012.

reforms.description: The government’s 2007 'Roadmap for Telecommunication Policy and Regulation' aims to encourage the development of new services through deregulation, competition, and greater predictability of regulation in the telecommunications sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering competition and innovation in telecommunications, the reform is expected to enhance service quality and lower prices, contributing to overall productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Roadmap was put into action to stimulate competition and innovation in the telecommunications sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in regulatory approach, aiming to enhance competition and innovation in a key sector of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s 2007 'Roadmap for Telecommunication Policy and Regulation' aims at encouraging the development of new services through deregulation, competition and greater predictability of regulation.

reforms.description: In February 2008, the Ministry of Information and Communication and the Korea Broadcasting Commission were integrated into the Korea Communications Commission, which regulates telecommunications, broadcasting, and convergence sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The creation of an independent regulatory body is expected to enhance the effectiveness of regulation and promote competition in the telecommunications and broadcasting sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The integration of regulatory bodies was completed to improve the regulatory framework for telecommunications and broadcasting.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the regulatory landscape, aiming to streamline oversight and enhance competition in converging sectors.
reforms.source_quote: In February 2008, the Ministry of Information and Communication and the Korea Broadcasting Commission were integrated into the Korea Communications Commission.

reforms.description: The government aims to establish an efficient reporting system for intellectual and intangible assets, alongside providing adequate protection of intellectual property to encourage investment in these areas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the protection of intellectual property, the reform is expected to stimulate investment in intangible assets, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to enhance the reporting system and protection of intellectual property has been outlined as a recommendation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards improving the investment climate for intangible assets, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Establish an efficient reporting system for intellectual and intangible assets and provide adequate protection of intellectual property to encourage investment in intangible assets.
